content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 1017 • SACRAMENTO 95814 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

May 12, 1978

Mr. Jeremiah F. Hallisey
O'Brien and Hallisey
One California Street
San Francisco, California 94111

Dear Mr. Hallisey:

Thank you for your letter of the [illegible] regarding our opinion or advice with respect to the possible violation by Mt. Carmel Rhodes, Mayor of Fremont, of the Political Reform Act and the Conflict of Interest provisions pursuant to Chapter 3.4, Government Code.

Based on our investigation of this matter, it would appear that Mr. Rhodes' possible violation of the Political Reform Act Statements: in particular, a potential failure to report from January 1, 1977, through March 14, 1978, a material interest in real property on File No. [illegible], 1978, through March 14, 1978 of office State Statement. Form 720, executed on March 15, 1978. The initial statement should have been filed the political timing April 3, 1978. The deadline for the filing of the office statement was April 7 and the address was [illegible] Office Statement was April 13.

Set forth below are the alternative ways in which Mr. Rhodes may satisfy his filing requirement. However, we wish to make it clear that Mr. Rhodes remains potentially liable for late filings as well as other sanctions with respect to all other through remedies of which alternative he exercises.

Alternative 1: Mr. Rhodes may file one Form 721 covering the period from January 1, 1977, through March 14, 1978.

Alternative 2: Mr. Rhodes may file two Statements -- a Form 721 covering the period from January 1, 1977, through March 8, 1978, and a Form 700 covering the period through March 14, 1978.

Mr. Jeremiah S. [illegible]
May 12, 1978
Page Two

Alternative 3: Mr. Rhodes may file three statements -- an
annual Statement, Form 721, covering the period from January 1,
1977, through December 31, 1977; a short office Statement, Form
Form 721, covering the period from January 1, 1978, through
March 8, 1978; and an assuming office statement, Form 720,
covering the date March 14, 1978.

On April 11, 1978, the Commission advised Mr. Rhodes that
he was subject to a fine for not filing his annual Statement
of Economic Interests. He was requested to file within ten (10)
days of receipt of notification and to provide the Commission
with a reason which might justify full or partial waiver of his
fine.

Since we have received no response from him to this date,
his full fine of $100 is due. He is therefore required to remit
promptly to this office his statement of Economic Interests and
a check or money order in the amount of $100, made payable to
the "State of California". The full amount will be deposited
into the General Fund of the State of California.

In addition, please be advised that we have also not
received Mr. Rhodes' leaving office statement and assuming office
Statement, as described above. Government Code Section 3103
provides that persons in the state officials and their deadlines
are liable in the manner of $10 for each day, to a maximum of $100.
Therefore, Mr. Rhodes is potentially liable for a fine of $100
for each of the three statements. Accordingly, discretion has been
exercised to fully or partially waive any fines if he provides
adequate reason for filing late and if the Statements are filed
within ten (10) days of the date of this letter with a written
explanation. This letter constitute specific written notice to Mr. Rhodes
of his filing obligations with respect to leaving office
Statement for April 7, 1978, and assuming office statement
due April 14, 1978.

Please contact us with any questions you may have.

Sincerely,

[illegible]
[illegible]
[illegible]

Tiered

State of California

Memorandum

To : Opinion Request Meeting Participants                Date : May 5, 1978


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Roger Brown

Subject: Letter from Jeremiah Hallisey Regarding Gene Rhodes


       The attached letter was received by the Commission
after Gene Rhodes was sued by local residents to compel
his filing of the required statement of economic interests.
In addition, Mr. Rhodes has received a mailgram from the
Conflicts Division notifying him that his annual statement
was due April 1, 1978, and several letters from the
Fremont City Manager's office notifying him of his
obligation to file by April 1, 1978.

       This matter is of some concern to the Enforcement
Division because we have been investigating a formal
complaint against Mr. Rhodes since March 13, 1978, which
alleged certain conflicts of interest and failure to dis-
close various economic interests.

       It is important that this matter be resolved as
expeditiously as possible.




RB:nc

Attachment

O'BRIEN AND HALLISEY

CHARLES A O'BRIEN
JEREMIAH F HALLISEY
CHARLENE HAUGHT JOHNSON
ROBERT E SHERMAN
ROBERT PATRICK MURPHY
DANIEL B. LEBAULT

ONE CALIFORNIA STREET
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94111

MAY 3 36 PM '78

415-433-8300

May 2, 1978


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, California 95814


Dear Members of the Commission:

This law firm represents Mayor Gene Rhodes of Fremont.

The purpose of this letter is to request a formal opinion, or

in the alternative, written advice, pursuant to Government Code,

§83114 and 2 Cal. Adm. Code, §18320 et seq., concerning the

duties and responsibilities of Gene Rhodes, Mayor of Fremont

with respect to the filing of statements of economic interest

pursuant to Government Code, §87200, et seq.

The material facts are as follows: Mr. Rhodes was,

at all times during calendar year 1977, a member of the City

Council of the City of Fremont. On or about March 30, 1977, he

filed, in a timely fashion, FPPC Form 721 covering the period

from March 9, 1976 to December 31, 1976. On or about December

29, 1977, along with his declaration of candidacy for the office

of Mayor of the City of Fremont, he filed FPPC Form 720 disclos-

ing certain investments and interests in real property as

required by Government Code, §87201.

Prior to this election, the office of Mayor of The

City of Fremont had been filled by vote of the City Council from

Fair Political Practices Commission
May 2, 1978
Page Two


among its members. Mr. Rhodes held that position for the years

1975 through 1977. By way of initiative action, the Office of

Mayor was made a distinct elective office, and a special election was held accordingly. Pursuant to this revision, the

newly elected mayor would continue to hold a voting position on

the City Council.

     The mayoralty election took place on March 7, 1978,

and Mr. Rhodes emerged the winner. He had previously stated

that regardless of the outcome of the election, he would immediately resign from the City Council in order that a successor

might also be chosen by special election. On March 8, he so

resigned effective that date. On March 14, 1978, he was sworn

in as Mayor.

     The basic question posed by these facts is this: Is

Mayor Rhodes required to comply with the provisions of Government

Code, §87204 with respect to his resignation from the City

Council, and Government Code, §87202 with respect to his assumption of the Office of Mayor? Or may he satisfy the filing

requirements of the Political Reform Act by complying with the

provisions of Government Code, §87203 in filing an annual statement for calendar year 1977?

     Government Code, §87205 provides:

     A person who completes a term of office
     specified in §87200 and on the same day begins
     a term of the same office or another such

Fair Political Practices Commission
May 2, 1978
Page Three


                office in the same jurisdiction is not deemed
                to assume office or leave office.

                The Commission has provided by Regulation, 2 Cal.

Adm. Code, §18723, that:

                If a person assumes an office specified in
                Government Code §87200 between October 1 and
                December 31 and files a Statement of Economic
                Interests pursuant to Government Code §87202,
                that person need not file a Statement of
                Economic Interests pursuant to §97203 until
                one year later than the date specified in
                subsection (b).

This regulation further provides that in certain circumstances,

a leaving office statement may be filed in lieu of an annual

statement.

            While neither of these provisions appears to directly

control the instant situation, read in conjunction they evidence

an intent to avoid unnecessary and/or repetitive filing of

documents which would disclose the same or much the same infor-

mation.  See also, 2 Cal. Adm. Code §13732 concerning filing

requirements of designated employees under Conflict of Interest

Codes.  At all times relevant to this matter, the "jurisdiction"

with respect to Mr. Rhodes' filing obligations was the City of

Fremont.  Thus, if it is the Commission's interpretation that

Mr. Rhodes neither left nor assumed office as Mayor under these

circumstances, it would appear that he may meet the requirements

of the cited provisions of the Government Code by filing an

annual statement (Form 721) covering the period of January 1,

Fair Political Practices Commission
May 2, 1978
Page Four


1977 to December 31, 1977.

Pending determination of this matter, Mr. Rhodes is,

with all deliberate speed, compiling the documents and informa-

tion necessary in order that the appropriate filings may be

made. Resolution of this question is, however, necessary to

enable him to comply with the filing requirements at the earliest

possible date. If you have any questions, please contact me.

Sincerely,


Jeremiah F. Hallisey


JFH/EL

cc: The Honorable Robert Nelson
    Assistant City Manager
    Civic Center
    Fremont, California

State of California

close various economic interests.

       It is important that this matter be resolved as
expeditiously as possible.

CHARLES A. O'BRIEN
JEREMIAH F. HALLISEY
CHARLENE HAUGHT JOHNSON
STANLEY S. TAYLOR, III
ROBERT PATRICK MURPHY
DANIEL B. LERAUD

LAW OFFICES

O'BRIEN AND HALLISEY
ONE CALIFORNIA STREET
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94111

415 433-5300

May 2, 1978


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, California  95814

          specified in §87200 and on the same day begins
          a term of the same office or another such

Fair Political Practices Commission
May 2, 1978
Page Three


office in the same jurisdiction is not deemed
to assume office or leave office.

The Commission has provided by Regulation, 2 Cal.

     subsection (b).

Mr. Rhodes neither left nor assumed office as Mayor under these

circumstances, it would appear that he may meet the requirements

of the cited provisions of the Government Code by filing an

annual statement (Form 721) covering the period of January 1,

Fair Political Practices Commission
May 2, 1978
Page Four


1977 to December 31, 1977.

Pending determination of this matter, Mr. Rhodes is,

with all deliberate speed, compiling the documents and informa-

tion necessary in order that the appropriate filings may be

Sincerely,


Jeremiah F. Hallisey


JFH/ci

cc:  The Honorable Robert Nelson
     Assistant City Manager
     Civic Center
     Fremont, California

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance: Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660                322-5601              322-6441              322-6444


January 23, 1978

                                                                                A78-253

Honorable Raymond L. Sullivan
Justice, Retired
Supreme Court of California
State Building, Room 4238
San Francisco, California 94102

Dear Justice Sullivan:

     This will confirm our telephone conversation of
January 23, 1978, during which I informed you that the
advice in Mr. Finney's January 28, 1977, letter is
still in effect. Retired judges sitting on assignment
in courts of record do not file Statements of Economic
Interests pursuant to the Political Reform Act.

     If you have any further questions, please feel
free to contact me.

                                                Sincerely,

                                                Barbara Campbell
                                                Counsel
                                                Legal Division

sections.question: Is Mayor Rhodes required to comply with the provisions of Government Code, §87204 with respect to his resignation from the City Council, and Government Code, §87202 with respect to his assumption of the Office of Mayor? Or may he satisfy the filing requirements of the Political Reform Act by complying with the provisions of Government Code, §87203 in filing an annual statement for calendar year 1977?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Gene Rhodes, upon leaving the Fremont City Council to assume the newly-elected office of Mayor, must file statements of economic interest under §87204 (leaving office) and §87202 (assuming office) separately, or whether he can satisfy filing requirements through a single annual statement under §87203 for calendar year 1977.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a law firm, not an FPPC response. The document includes a memorandum from FPPC staff (Roger Brown) summarizing the matter for opinion request meeting participants. The quality of the source document is poor with significant OCR degradation, but the main request letter portion is legible. No FPPC conclusion/advice is present in this document.

embedding.summary: A law firm representing Mayor Gene Rhodes of Fremont requests FPPC guidance on whether Rhodes must file separate leaving-office and assuming-office statements after resigning from the City Council to become Mayor in March 1978, or whether a single annual statement for 1977 satisfies the Political Reform Act requirements.
